title: Understanding Bulk Modulus
text: The bulk modulus (K) is a measure of a material's resistance to uniform compression. It is defined as the ratio of the applied pressure increase to the relative decrease in volume.

title: Formula for Fractional Volume Change
text: The fractional change in volume $$\frac{\Delta V}{V}$$ can be calculated using the formula: $$\frac{\Delta V}{V}=\frac{-\Delta P}{K}$$, where ΔP is the change in pressure and K is the bulk modulus.

title: Calculate the Fractional Volume Change
text: Given that the bulk modulus $$K=1\times{10}^{11}$$ N/m² and the pressure change $$ΔP={10}^7$$ N/m², use the formula $$\frac{\Delta V}{V}=\frac{-\Delta P}{K}$$ to find the fractional volume change.
